title: Platform Adapter Management
subtitle: Manage bot platform adapters to connect to different chat platforms
emptyText: No platform adapters yet, click Add Adapter to create one

webhookDialog.title: Webhook Callback URL
webhookDialog.description: The callback address is as follows, please ensure that the network environment can be publicly accessible. You can also view the callback address information in the logs. It is recommended to fill in the "Externally Accessible Callback API Address" in Settings -> General.

idConflict.title: ID Conflict Warning
idConflict.message: Detected duplicate ID "{id}". Please use a new ID.

securityWarning.title: Security Warning
securityWarning.aiocqhttpTokenMissing: To secure reverse WebSocket connections, it is strongly recommended to set ws_reverse_token. Leaving it empty may introduce security risks.
securityWarning.napcatTokenMissing: To secure the NapCat forward WebSocket connection, it is strongly recommended to set token. Leaving it empty may introduce security risks.

createDialog.step1Hint: Where do you want to connect the bot? e.g. QQ, WeCom, Feishu, Discord, Telegram.
createDialog.configHint: How do you want to configure the bot? The config file includes model, persona, knowledge base, plugins, and more.
createDialog.configDefaultHint: Uses the default config file "default" by default. You can configure it later.
createDialog.newConfigLoadFailed: Failed to load default config template
createDialog.noRouteRules: No route rules for this platform. The default config file will be used.

routeSource.noData: No unified message source for this bot is available yet. It will appear here after this bot receives at least one message from the corresponding messaging platform.

createDialog.routeHint: When delivering messages, the first matching config file from top to bottom is used based on session source. Use * to match all. Use /session info to get the session ID. If none match, the default config file is used.

configTableHeaders.configId: Config ID linked to this instance

messages.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete platform adapter
messages.configNotFoundOpenConfig: Target config file not found. Opened the config page for review.
messages.configIdMissing: Unable to get config file ID.

quickActions.title: Platform Quick Actions
quickActions.description: Run proactive management actions against this platform instance directly. Nothing here is saved as a template; each submission is a one-off platform action call.
quickActions.noAction: This platform does not expose any proactive actions.

help.send_poke: Provide user_id to run the action. Leave group_id empty for a private poke; set group_id for a group poke.
help.send_like: times defaults to 1 and can be increased as needed.
help.send_group_notice: Most cases only need group_id and content. Fill image, pinned, and other fields only when needed.
help.kick_group_members: user_ids accepts comma-separated or newline-separated values and will be converted into an array automatically.
help.delete_essence_message: Usually message_id is enough. Only fill msg_seq, msg_random, or group_id when your platform flow specifically requires them.

fields.group_idHintOptional: Optional. Leaving it empty only works for actions that can infer it from context.
fields.user_idsHint: Use commas or new lines to separate multiple user IDs.
fields.target_idHintOptional: Optional. Some platforms use this as the poke target.
fields.messageIdHintOptional: Optional. Leave empty when the platform can infer it from context.
fields.imageHintOptional: Optional. Used for group notice images.
fields.msgSeqHintOptional: Optional. Use only when message_id is not suitable.
fields.msgRandomHintOptional: Optional. Use only when message_id is not suitable.
